resources.string: Select your role on the court to personalize your training
Team organizer. Ball handling, vision and speed.
Outside scorer. Shooting and driving to the basket.
Versatile player. Shooting, rebounding and perimeter defense.
Inside and outside game. Strength and mid-range shooting.
Dominates under the rim. Post play, rebounding and interior defense.
No videos available for this role
Practice dribbling alternating right and left hand. Keep your head up and eyes forward. Do 3 sets of 30 seconds per hand.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees, align your elbow with the basket and release the ball with a smooth wrist motion. Do 20 repetitions.
Adopt the defensive stance with bent knees, straight back and extended arms. Move laterally following the attacker without crossing your feet.
Hold the ball with both hands at chest height. Extend your arms toward your teammate and release the ball with a wrist snap. Do 3 sets of 15 repetitions.
Reference video with fundamental point guard movements: ball handling on the move, direction changes, court vision and quick decision making.
Coordinate with the center to use the screen. After the screen, decide whether to drive, shoot or pass to the roller based on the defense reaction.
Alternate between fast and slow dribble to unbalance the defender. Use the speed change when the defender drops their guard.
Dribbling on the move and shooting guard drills
Dribbling drills combined with shooting guard technical moves: crossover, between the legs, behind the back and finishes at the rim.
Prepare your shot before receiving the ball. As soon as you catch it, shoot directly without extra dribbles. The goal is to reduce your release time.
Fake going one direction and cut sharply toward the rim or open space. The goal is to get open from your defender to receive the pass.
On a drive, shoot a soft shot over the interior defender when you can\'t reach the rim with a regular layup. Practice from both sides.
Reference video with key small forward movements: mid-range shooting, off-ball movement, cuts to the rim, offensive rebounding and perimeter defense.
Anticipate the shot trajectory and position yourself between the defender and the rim. Jump with both feet and catch the ball at its highest point.
Work on defensive positioning on the perimeter. Keep proper distance from the attacker and react to both the dribble and the pass.
Post moves adapted to the modern power forward: spins, finishes near the rim, footwork and how to gain advantage without being the tallest player.
After the opponent\'s shot, box out the attacker and jump for the ball. Catch with two hands and protect it from the opponent on landing.
Work the three-point shot as an additional weapon. Practice from the corners and from the top, catching in motion.
Reference video with essential center moves under the rim: back-to-basket post play, hook shots with both hands and finishes close to the basket.
From the low post, take a lateral step away from the defender and release the ball with an arcing arm motion over the defender.
Work on defensive zone positioning. Keep your arms up, anticipate the pass and jump to block without committing a personal foul.
You have no favorite drills yet
Do you want to change your position on the court?
An error occurred. Please try again.
